title: calpidia rostrata
intro: Calpidia rostrata, commonly known as the desert marigold, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is known for its bright yellow and orange flowers, which bloom in the desert regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Calpidia rostrata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic actions in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and therapeutic actions.
side_effects: Calpidia rostrata can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems in some individuals. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extract has been linked to cardiovascular complications, including hypertension and arrhythmia. Additionally, using calpidia rostrata without proper guidance can cause interactions with other medications, leading to adverse reactions. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using calpidia rostrata for any health benefits to avoid these potential side effects.
